commands.review_summary:
Routes manual `/approve` submissions through `resolveApprovalOverGateway` for plugin approval ids, with focused approval-command tests plus slash-command docs and changelog updates.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible, but not live reproduced here: current main directly calls the gateway from the command handler while docs and plugin approval IDs establish plugin approvals as a supported `/approve` target. The PR body includes prior Slack failure text but no live artifact.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer handling for the protected label and external-pr real behavior proof; no narrow ClawSweeper repair is indicated because the code review found no definite patch defect.

target.title: fix(slack): resolve manual plugin approvals through runtime
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `/approve plugin:<id>` from Slack/other channel commands could call the raw gateway route directly, so active plugin approvals were reported as unknown or expired instead of resolving through the runtime that owns in-memory approval state.
- Solution: route `/approve` submissions through `resolveApprovalOverGateway`, using the plugin resolve method for `plugin:` ids while preserving the existing exec/plugin fallback behavior.
- What changed: updated the approve command handler, refreshed focused tests around resolver calls and plugin routing, and documented that `/approve` handles exec and plugin approvals.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): approval authorization policy, approver config shape, native approval delivery, and approval id formats are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Slack plugin approval prompts need a manual `/approve plugin:<id> <decision>` path that reaches the same trusted approval runtime as native approval actions. The raw gateway call path could miss active plugin approvals and return `unknown or expired approval id`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: manual channel `/approve plugin:<id>` should resolve pending plugin approvals instead of returning `unknown or expired approval id`.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS/Darwin arm64, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 18 passed (18)`.
- Observed result after fix: focused tests confirm plugin approvals are submitted through `resolveApprovalOverGateway` with `resolveMethod: "plugin"`, while exec approvals and legacy fallback behavior still route correctly.
- What was not tested: live Slack slash-command approval was not rerun from this split worktree.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): prior manual Slack `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` returned `Failed to submit approval: unknown or expired approval id`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the `/approve` command bypassed the approval runtime and called gateway approval methods directly, so plugin approvals stored behind the runtime resolver could be invisible to manual slash-command submission.
- Missing detection / guardrail: tests asserted raw gateway method calls instead of verifying the same resolver path used by native approval actions.
- Contributing context (if known): plugin approvals and exec approvals have different backing managers, and plugin ids need explicit plugin resolution instead of a raw default gateway resolve attempt.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` calls the trusted approval resolver with plugin routing and still enforces configured approvers.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression was in command routing, before any live Slack delivery behavior.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing approve-command tests covered parsing/auth and are updated to assert the resolver path.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Manual `/approve` commands now support plugin approval prompts as documented, instead of being limited to exec approvals.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
/slack /approve plugin:<id> -> raw gateway resolve -> plugin approval may be missed -> unknown/expired

After:
/slack /approve plugin:<id> -> approval runtime resolver -> plugin approval manager -> resolved
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Darwin 25.2.0 arm64
- Runtime/container: local worktree, Node v24.15.0, pnpm 11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Slack manual `/approve` command path; verified with focused command-handler tests
- Relevant config (redacted): channel plugin approval approver config exercised in tests

### Steps

1. Submit `/approve plugin:<id> allow-once` from a configured approver.
2. Handler authorizes the sender for plugin approval.
3. Handler calls `resolveApprovalOverGateway` with plugin routing.

### Expected

- Pending plugin approval resolves or the actual resolver error is returned.

### Actual

- Focused tests pass and assert plugin approval routing through the resolver.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: focused approve-command test suite with exec approvals, plugin approvals, fallback routing, authorization, and gateway-client scopes.
- Edge cases checked: unauthorized plugin approvers remain rejected; legacy unprefixed fallback still attempts exec then plugin; plugin-only not-found errors surface cleanly.
- What you did **not** verify: live Slack approval roundtrip from this branch.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`): Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`): No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: changing the manual command path could regress exec approval submission.
  - Mitigation: focused tests assert exec approval routing still submits successfully and preserve existing fallback behavior.
target.author: kevinslin
